_status:
DRAFT — review artifact, not loaded by dialogue_runner. Per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1, reshapes the Crab states so Crab provides observations and Cula synthesises. Builds on top of the in-flight voice-pack rewrite (data/dialogues/crab.json worktree state) — same state IDs, same address forms, same `once: true` discipline, same `on_dismiss` flag writes. The substantive change is in the LINE CONTENT and the OPTIONS-BLOCK SEMANTICS: option text now represents the synthesis Cula proposes (one well-fitted, two not-well-fitted), `write_path` is `chapter1.proposed_frame` (string enum declared at SAVE_VERSION 17, see state.gd), and Crab's response in `crab_post_pitch_response` is calibrated by frame-fit rather than universal-recruitment. Recruitment still happens for all three branches; the variance is in what kind of co-counsel Crab has just become.

Merge strategy: when ready, the human (or Design role) takes this draft, opens the live crab.json, replaces ONLY the bodies of before_binder / before_binder_briefing / first_meeting_with_binder / after_binder_first_engagement / crab_post_pitch_response. All other states (hint_needs_archive, crab_hint_court_ready, after_engagement, the four coffee_reaction states, idle_flavor) remain unchanged from the in-flight voice-pack rewrite.

Voice discipline: every line audited against narrative_revision/voice_agents/crab.md (gear-shift register) and narrative_revision/ai_voice_constraints.md §1 (no em-dash stacking, no contrastive antithesis except canonical 'postal theatre' anchor, no scrub-list vocabulary). The 'postal theatre' line moves OUT of first_meeting_with_binder (where it currently lands as Crab's conclusion) and BECOMES the wrong_shape_correction Crab offers when Cula picks the wrong frame in option C — the same line, now landing as Crab's diagnosis of Cula's mistake rather than Crab's diagnosis of the case.
_proposal_anchor: PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1 (Crab subsection) + §2 (synthesis-via-dialogue-options) + §3 (proposed_frame enum + binder_read_* read-state flags) + §4 (soft-fail).
_design_role_handoff: All `_status: DESIGN_TODO` line slots need final voicing. The line shapes I've drafted are placeholders that demonstrate the reshape; final voice belongs to Design.

states._comment: RESHAPED. Crab observes; he does not conclude. The envelope-fact is split — Crab notices the discrepancy between two numbers but does not name what the discrepancy means. 'Postal theatre' line removed from here (it appears now only in crab_post_pitch_response's wrong-shape correction). Cula leaves with the binder errand still open; the synthesis has not started.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Heard. You've been at the binder?

Mm. Look at the brass plate above us. Number twelve. The copy of the eviction envelope I have seen — number seven. The two numbers belong to the same staircase. I do not know yet what the file says about that. The file is in the binder.

states._comment: RESHAPED. Repeat pre-binder visit. Crab names the renumbering as a fact about the building (not about the case). The hint pushes Cula to the binder but does not preview the answer.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Still no binder.
The block was renumbered. Some years back. The dates and the addresses live in the binder. I am not the one who reads the dates. Bring it down.

states._comment: RESHAPED — LOAD-BEARING. Crab takes the binder, reads aloud what is on each relevant page (envelope address, renewal address), names the renumbering year. He does NOT name the Article. He does NOT name the doctrine. He turns the synthesis to Cula with a question. The options block is now Cula's synthesis — three argument FRAMES she can propose, with write_path = chapter1.proposed_frame (a v17 string enum declared in state.gd). All three values flip recruited_crab to true via on_dismiss; the substantive difference is which frame Cula committed to, which the court-round controller will read at Phase 2 start. Three on_dismiss `set` actions fire alongside: binder_read_envelope, binder_read_renewal, binder_read_renumbering — recording that Cula has now SEEN these evidence cards regardless of which frame she picked.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. Heard. Hand it here.

Page one. Envelope of the notice — addressed to number seven. Dated the eighth of April. Page two — the two thousand and nineteen renewal — addressed to number twelve. Landlord's countersignature on the page facing. Cross-reference page three — block was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen.
Three pages. One landlord. Two addresses. What is the shape of the argument, Dr. A. Cula.

choices.text: The notice went to the wrong door. He knew. That is the argument.
He has no right to evict her — she's had the lease since eighty-seven.
Friday is close. We file what we have and argue the procedure on the day.

states._comment: RESHAPED — late-binder path (Cula spoke to Crab pre-binder, then went and got the binder). Same shape as first_meeting_with_binder — synthesis-choice options with frame-id values — minus the greeting (Crab knows Cula now). The same three on_dismiss binder-read flags fire.
lines: Page one — envelope to number seven, eighth of April. Page two — renewal to number twelve, two thousand and nineteen, countersignature. Page three — renumbering, two thousand and fifteen.
What is the shape, Dr. A. Cula.

choices.text: He served the wrong door. Knowingly. That is the case.
Argue the underlying tenancy. He cannot evict her — sixty-two lease, eighty-seven inheritance.
We file the motion. The defect is on the face of the envelope.

states._comment:
RESHAPED. Crab's response is now CALIBRATED BY frame-fit. The well-fitted frame (defective_service_135bis) earns Crab attaching the legal citation — 'Article one-thirty-five-bis, paragraph two of the Code of Civil Procedure' — to Cula's named shape. The wrong-shape frame (merits_defence) earns the 'postal theatre' line as Crab's diagnosis of Cula's mistake, with a quiet walk-back. Both paths recruit Crab and proceed; the soft-fail axis is in chapter1.judicial_patience starting lower if Cula carries the wrong frame into court (controller wiring deferred to Phase 2 controller restoration). Address form switches to bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md — recruitment beat unlocks it.

Note: the trigger gates on proposed_frame != '' so this fires only after the chain has set the enum. Two response-variant states would be the Halina-style 'tier-gated response' pattern, but to keep this draft narrow I've fused them into one state with two _option_response sub-blocks marked by inline triggers. If the dialogue runner does not support inline-conditional lines, Design splits this into two sibling states (crab_post_pitch_response_fit and crab_post_pitch_response_wrong) at merge time.
states._design_role_handoff: Decide at merge time whether to (a) split into two sibling states for the two frame-fit branches, or (b) leave as one state and have the dialogue runner skip un-matching inline groups. The split is cleaner under the existing runner; the inline approach is what's drafted here for illustration. Adopt option (a) unless runner support is added.
lines: Cula. Right. The shape is wrong-door service. Article one-thirty-five-bis, paragraph two of the Code of Civil Procedure. He sent a writ to a building that has not been Mrs. Sikorska's building in eleven years. A confession with a postal date.

Drafting takes an afternoon. The walking takes Friday. I will take the post — register, return-to-sender slip, the resident at number seven. Find me before fourteen hundred if anything in the binder reads wrong.

states._comment:
RESHAPED — sibling state for the wrong-shape frame. Per the _design_role_handoff note on crab_post_pitch_response, this is the recommended split. Trigger is more specific (proposed_frame == 'merits_defence'), so JSON-order priority places this BEFORE the generic crab_post_pitch_response. Crab walks Cula back without dead-ending: the merits frame survives, the chapter still progresses, but Cula has now heard the 'postal theatre' line as the correction. The court-round controller will read proposed_frame and calibrate Phase 2 accordingly.

NB: the live crab.json's existing 'postal theatre' line in first_meeting_with_binder must be removed at merge time — it migrates here.
lines: Cula. The tenancy is not before the court at the motion. That comes later, if it comes.

Look at the envelope again. Number seven. Look at the renewal. Number twelve, two thousand and nineteen, landlord's countersignature. He knew which door. He sent it to the other one. That is not service. That is postal theatre.
We can still file. We file on the service defect. The merits stay where they are.

idle_flavor: The office is quiet. Either nothing has happened or the evidence is hiding better than usual.
Half the legal questions in this office begin with someone reading the address on an envelope twice.
